title: Why the Toronto Raptors still very much have a type when it comes to draft prospects & past 9th picks gone wrong | Mailbag!

summary: The Toronto Raptors have long been pegged as having a clear type of prospect they dig in the NBA Draft, but do they still actually target the classic "Raptorsy" prospects they were once known for? In Episode 1871, Sean Woodley goes solo to dig into some NBA Draft-themed mailbag questions from the listeners! Next, Sean gets into the last fourth ninth-overall picks -- Zach Edey, Taylor Hendricks, Jeremy Sochan and Davion Mitchell -- and discusses where those teams went wrong, if they did at all. title: Marčiulionio palikimas Lietuvai, dėl kurio nubraukėme ašarą
summary: Šarūnas Marčiulionis „Off the Record“ tinklalaidėje su Martynu Pociumi ir Donatu Urbonu pasakoja apie vaikystės nelaimę, dėl kurios jo karjera galėjo užsibaigti nė neprasidėjusi, kuo neįtiko Kauno „Žalgiriui“ ir kaip dėl to jautėsi, kokią didžiulę paslaptį jis nešėsi į savo NBA etapą, „nuteisėjautą“ 1995-ųjų Europos čempionato finalą, į Vilnių atvežtą „Apple“ įkūrėją, palikimą Lietuvai, dėl kurio podkaste riedėjo ašaros, ir bręstantį sugrįžimą į Lietuvos krepšinį. Tinklalaidės partneriai: – Telia Play pristato dokumentiką „Pionieriai“. Tai penkių dalių serialas apie Marčiulionį ir Volkovą. Dokumentika pasiekiama visiems užsisakius Telia Play + filmų ir serialų prenumeratą. Daugiau informacijos čia. – Artroklinika – atraskite regeneracinės medicinos privalumus! Mūsų inovatyvios procedūros padeda išvengti operacijų ir užtikrina greitą atsistatymą. Užpildykite formą, o klinikos komanda susisieks ir suteiks visą reikiamą informaciją. – InSoil Finance – lyderiaujanti tvaraus investavimo platforma! Registruokitės ir su kodu BASKETNEWS10 iki gegužės 25 d. gaukite 10 EUR premiją pirmai investicijai. Temos:  Ką reiškia Marčiulionio šūkis, kurį Pocius atsimena iš vaikystės? (0:00); Kaip sprogmuo vos neužbaigė Šaro karjeros jai dar net neprasidėjus (7:51); Apie Marčiulionį tarp Lietuvos vaikų sklandžiusi legenda (13:42); Mitai ir tikrovė: kodėl Marčiulionis neįtiko „Žalgiriui“? (16:06); Apie karjeros pabaigą galvojusio Marčiulionio patarimas tiems, kas nuleidžia rankas (21:38); To nežinojo ir pats Pocius: kaip Šaras bandė pakeisti Martyno sprendimą (29:30); Momentas, po kurio apie Marčiulionį sužinojo pasaulis (39:56); Marčiulionio triukas kritinėmis akimirkomis ir akistata su geriausiu (44:15); Marčiulionio reakcija išgirdus savo pavardę NBA naujokų biržoje (54:53); Jei ne šitai, nebūtų ir Seulo aukso? (1:00:53); Suplėšytas pirmas Marčiulionio NBA kontraktas (1:08:09); Kaip Marčiulionis tapo eurostepo pradininku NBA (1:13:53); Marčiulionio paslaptis, kurią jis slėpė nuo NBA žmonių (1:22:00); Marčelos spindesys prie Jordano, Magico ir Birdo (1:35:01); Karjeros mačas prieš Draženą Petrovičių (1:44:20); Kaip Marčela bandė uždengti Jordaną (1:45:33); Kaip niekas net neturėjo pamatyti legendinių Lietuvos rinktinės marškinėlių (1:51:47); Lietuva prieš „Svajonių komandą“ ir pergalė, be kurios krepšinis būtų kitoks (1:59:18); Pamatykite, kuo ypatinga buvo Marčiulionio akademija (2:06:07); Kaip Apple įkūrėjas į Vilnių vežė kompiuterius ir idėjininkai, dėl kurių dabar rieda ašaros (2:13:43); Marčiulionis planuoja grįžti į Lietuvos krepšinį? (2:19:08); Kaip Marčiulionis įkūrė LKL su naujokų birža ir NBA taisyklių idėja (2:28:07); Kaip Marčiulionis nešė Ameriką į Lietuvą mafijos laikais (2:35:18); Marčiulionio akademijos istorijos ir pavardė, kurią Šarūnas su ašaromis nešasi iki šiol (2:45:09); Gyvenimo prasmė pagal Marčiulionį (2:51:33); Kaip kai kurie Lietuvos projektai „atstūmė“ Marčiulionį (2:56:34); Kaip Marčiulionis Magicą Johnsoną prikalbino į savo lygą (3:03:20); Kaip savi lietuviai sugriovė Marčiulionio „FIBA Europe“ prezidento planus (3:06:28); Ar Lietuva pati atstūmė Šarūną Marčiulionį? title: WNBA Rivalries, Sneaker Drops, and Tunnel Style + Paige Bueckers’ Stylist Brittany Hampton interview
podcast_name: No Offseason: The Athletic Women's Basketball Show
summary: In this episode, we’re stepping off the court and into the culture – to discuss all the ways in which the WNBA, and women’s basketball in general, impact our society at large. We’re talking: what’s the purpose of rivalries? Do they exist in the W? Is Caitlin vs Angel even real? And we’re also talking style, fashion, and brands – downloading on the Met Gala, those iconic A’ja Wilson A’One ads, and the W references popping up all over TV. Then, Zena chats with stylist and fashion designer, Brittany Hampton, who shares the inside story on crafting Paige Bueckers’ looks for the recent WNBA Draft, as well as her takes on the fits from the Met Gala and the unique fire of WNBA Tunnel Style. _________________ Visit our YouTube page! _________________ References: WNBA 2025 Rivalry Week Match-ups WNBA players, WNBPA say Cathy Engelbert’s comments on Clark-Reese rivalry were a ‘fumble’ by Ben Pickman and Lukas Weese A’ja Wilson A’One Nike Ads: Here and Here Brittany Hampton featured in: Paige Bueckers: A Basketball Star is Born by Abigail Covington _________________ Get in touch: NoOffseason@theathletic.com Follow us on social media: -Instagram: @tanooffseason -TikTok: @no_offseason -Zena on BlueSky: @zenakeita -Ben on BlueSky: @benpickman -Sabreena on BlueSky: @sabreenajm Visit the Yahoo Women’s Sports Hub  Hosted on Acast. title: Blown leads, series adjustments & NBA Draft Lottery preview
summary: KOC and Tom Haberstroh react to the Wolves evening their series with the Golden State Warriors at 1-1. Anthony Edwards suffered a bad-looking ankle injury, but miraculously returned for the 2nd half. But does it even matter, as Kevin thinks the Warriors are DONE after this one? PLUS - what team would be the absolute dream landing spot for Cooper Flagg on Monday night's NBA Draft Lottery? The guys preview the Lotto that is just a weekend away!   Up next, NBA Skills Coach Drew Hanlen is back on The KOC Show to talk about how his client Chet Holmgren bounced back to have a great Game 2. What did the Oklahoma City Thunder do different on both ends of the floor to completely neutralize Nikola Jokic? Hanlen also works with Tyrese Haliburton of the Indiana Pacers, calling him "the best passer in the NBA." With the Pacers set to take on the Cavs in Game 3, KOC wants to know, why doesn't he shoot more??   And then, Noa Dalzell and Jack Simone join KOC to break down how the Boston Celtics have fallen into the completely unexpected 0-2 hole vs the underdog New York Knicks. How much of the blame should superstar Jayson Tatum get, and how much of it falls on HC Joe Mazzulla? Find out the answers to all those questions in this awesome KOC Show conversation!   (0:22) Tom Haberstroh joins the show (0:33) Wolves beat Steph-less Warriors in Game 2 (18:41) NBA Draft Lottery preview (35:33) Drew Hanlen joins the show (36:06) Thunder dominate Nuggets in Game 2 (55:10) Pacers take 2-0 lead vs. Cavs (1:11:57) Noa Dalzell & Jack Simone join the show (1:12:32) Celtics blow ANOTHER 20-pt lead to Knicks Subscribe to The Kevin O’Connor Show on your favorite podcast app: 🎧 Apple Podcasts 🎧 Spotify 🖥️ YouTube 📢 Check out the Yahoo Sports podcast network

title: Another Celtics Collapse STUNS The Gil's Arena Crew
summary: Gil's Arena Reacts To Another STUNNING Boston Celtics Collapse as Gilbert Arenas & The Gil's Arena Crew break down another 20 point meltdown for the Boston Celtics in the NBA Playoffs and discuss what went wrong for Jayson Tatum, Jaylen Brown and the rest of the Celtics as they choked away Game 2 to the New York Knicks. They analyze how Jalen Brunson, Mikal Bridges and Karl Anthony-Towns closed out the reigning champions in epic fashion 2 games in a row and debate if the Celtics will change their high volume shooting strategy to try and get back into this series. They then move over to the Western Conference where the Gil's Arena Crew breaks down the OKC Thunder's beatdown of the Denver Nuggets in Game 2 and explains how Shai Gilgeous Alexander was able to change his game and conquer Nikola Jokic in the 2nd game of the series. Finally, they react to Gregg Poppovich announcing his retirement from the NBA and discuss how the legendary head coach of the San Antonio Spurs changed the game of basketball before discussing Draymond Green calling out Dillon Brooks and saying nobody in the NBA respects the Houston Rockets villain. title: Caitlin Clark is BACK Plus Our Met Gala Favs
podcast_name: A Touch More with Sue Bird & Megan Rapinoe
summary: This week we’re zooming in on the W’s preseason, including Paige Bueckers’ debut with Dallas and way-too-early odds on MVP rankings. Plus, Caitlin Clark is back and outdoing herself already. We also take a closer look at the ACL tears that are plaguing the league, and share our thoughts on a new FIFA-study investigating how menstrual cycles contribute to ACL tears. On a lighter note, we ogle the eye-popping suiting at the 2025 Met Gala, which included some of our favorite athletes. Finally, Sue has a new podcast! Bird’s Eye View will be getting into the weeds of the x’s and o’s of women’s basketball. title: Tyrese Haliburton's BIG SHOT Blows Up Gil's Arena!!
summary: Tyrese Haliburton's BIG SHOT Blows Up Gil's Arena as Gilbert Arenas & The Gil's Arena Crew react to Tyrese Haliburton continuing his revenge tour after being called the most overrated player in the NBA by sinking a clutch shot in the NBA Playoffs to give his Indiana Pacers the Game 2 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers. They break down what this stunning comeback means for the rest of the season and debate if the Cavs are cooked after injuries to Evan Mobley & Darius Garland have left Donovan Mitchell fighting on his own. They then react to the Golden State Warriors pulling off a Game One win against the Minnesota Timberwolves series despite Steph Curry going down with a multi-game injury. They discuss what this injury means to the series moving forward and debate if Playoff Jimmy Butler & the red hot Buddy Hield can carry this squad and outplay Anthony Edwards. Finally, they react to the media circus surrounding James Harden after the Los Angeles Clippers exit from the NBA Playoffs and debate if it's fair to call him the biggest playoff choker in NBA History before, giving Gregg Popovich his flowers after the legendary head coach announced his retirement from coaching.
